preview_text:
The model favors Turkey at 38%, with the USA at 31% and a draw at 31%. The underdog role falls to the United States, who trail the favorite by roughly 7 percentage points in win probability, though the gap is modest enough that all three outcomes remain genuinely competitive.

The spread across outcomes reveals a tight match. The model assigns nearly equal weight to a USA victory and a draw—both in the 31% range—while Turkey's slight edge reflects a marginal structural advantage. No outcome commands majority expectation, indicating the fixture is expected to be closely contested with genuine uncertainty about the result.

The sportsbook consensus diverges from the model in meaningful ways. Books price the USA 6 percentage points higher (37% vs. 31%) and assign draws 4 points lower (27% vs. 31%). The market treats this as slightly less even, favoring the underdog more strongly than the model does while reducing draw likelihood. Polymarket prices align more closely with sportsbooks than with the model on all three outcomes. The dashboard does not claim to know which source's assessment will prove more accurate.
